session_note: S81 — playtest round-3 fixes. USER EXPLICIT batch approval: 'lets run all those edits ive just mentioned!' (7 user-dictated edits). priorities.label: S81 P1 — splat wipe is OWNER-ONLY: only the structure owner's avatar cleans a structure-splat (enemy walk-over no longer wipes)
priorities.check_method: S81 P1: canAvatarCleanSplat pure predicate in seagullLifecycle (SPLAT_STRUCTURE + live anchor + not-benched [S80 invariant folded in] + ownerColor===player.color + POOP_CLEAN_RADIUS sq-dist); main.ts sweep inner loop replaced with the predicate (orphan branch untouched; POOP_CLEAN_RADIUS import dropped from main). Ownership via ownerColor — rainbow shuffle remaps player.color + prim ownerColor in lockstep (parity test added); components single-owner per S46 P3 bond segregation. GATES: tsc -b --noEmit clean; seagull.test.ts 20/20 (+6: owner-in/enemy-in/owner-out/bench-expiry/rainbow-parity/orphan+falling); FULL vitest 1176/1176 (62 files). Commit 2173a04 pushed.
priorities.objective: User: 'a spark should not be able to wipe off his enemies structure only his own.' Gate the main.ts avatar-proximity CLEAN_POOP sweep on ownership of the splat's anchor prim (ownerColor === player.color — survives rainbow shuffle). Orphan sweep stays unconditional.
priorities.scope: src/main.ts (sweep gate), src/state/seagulls/seagullLifecycle.ts (pure exported predicate + docs), seagull.test.ts (+tests)
priorities.testing: vitest unit on the predicate (owner cleans / enemy does not / benched does not / orphan unaffected); tsc; local seagull e2e if touched behavior is covered there

verification.needle: an ENEMY within radius may NOT clean

priorities.label: S81 P2 — hot potato: carrying the potato >3s cooks it off IN HAND (per-grab timer; re-grab resets)
priorities.check_method: S81 P2: POTATO_HOLD_DETONATE_TICKS=180 (3s); Potato.carriedAtTick stamped in applyPickupPotato (re-grab restamps = pass restarts), cleared in applyPlacePotato+applyDropPotato; pure shouldCookOffInHand(potato,tick) exported + used by the main.ts poll (dispatches POTATO_DETONATE → existing carrier-bench path); save.ts SerializedPotato.carriedAtTick additive-optional (emitted only while CARRIED → FREE/ARMED snapshot bytes unchanged; deserialize passes through). E2E coupling pre-checked: grab→place carry legs are sub-3s sim; the S75 bench spec (fuse 180 = hold 180) has identical observable. GATES: tsc clean; vitest 1181/1181 (+5: stamp/boundary, place-clears, drop-clears, re-grab-restarts, cook-off benches); LOCAL playwright potato.spec chromium 4/4 (29.5s). Commit bc769d8 pushed.
priorities.objective: User: held it 22s then dumped at the last second ('hogging'). Add POTATO_HOLD_DETONATE_TICKS = 3s: a CARRIED potato detonates 3s after pickup (existing carrier-bench path punishes). Each grab restarts the 3s window (pass-it-on gameplay). From-spawn fuse (FREE dissipate / ARMED detonate) unchanged.
priorities.scope: src/constants.ts, src/state/potato.ts (carriedAtTick), src/state/potatoLifecycle.ts (pickup sets / place+drop clear), src/main.ts (poll), src/state/save.ts (optional field round-trip), potatoLifecycle.test.ts
priorities.testing: vitest: 3s cook-off fires + benches carrier; place/drop before 3s does NOT; re-grab resets window; serialization round-trip. Check e2e potato spec for hold-duration coupling BEFORE constant lands; add __TEST_ seam only if e2e needs it

priorities.label: S81 P3 — seagull poops at RANDOM intervals (different every pass), not a fixed metronome
priorities.check_method: S81 P3: POOP_DROP_INTERVAL_TICKS (fixed 33) → POOP_DROP_MIN/MAX_TICKS (12/48, avg ~30 preserves per-pass density); per-drop gap = murmur3-finalizer mix32(seagullId, lastPoopTick) % span + MIN — STATELESS randomness (zero RNG-stream consumption → 5 spawner streams byte-identical; zero new gull fields; both hash inputs already ride save/load → replay + host-reload reproduce the identical pattern). Stale FIXED-interval docs swept in seagull.ts/seagullLifecycle.ts/constants.ts/test header. seagull.spec smoke wait 1500→2500ms (worst-case first gap 48 ticks at degraded CI fps; was sized for the 33-tick metronome). GATES: tsc clean; seagull.test.ts 21/21 (sim sweep: bounded gaps + >1 distinct + >4 drops/400 ticks; pure-fn sweep: 601-tick range/purity + per-gull divergence); FULL vitest 1182/1182; LOCAL playwright seagull.spec chromium 1/1. Commit d56cfcf pushed.
priorities.objective: Replace fixed POOP_DROP_INTERVAL_TICKS with POOP_DROP_MIN/MAX_TICKS sampled per-drop via a pure integer hash of (seagullId, lastPoopTick) — deterministic + replay-safe + save/load-safe with ZERO new state and no RNG-stream changes.
priorities.scope: src/constants.ts, src/state/seagulls/seagullLifecycle.ts (hash + interval fn), seagull.test.ts (update fixed-interval tests; add range/variability/determinism tests)
priorities.testing: vitest: all sampled intervals within [MIN,MAX]; same inputs → same interval; consecutive drops differ (variability); seagull e2e spec checked for interval coupling

priorities.label: S81 P4 — remote (enemy) cruisers render SMOOTH: exponential interpolation of avatarPos instead of raw 10Hz steps
priorities.check_method: S81 P4: AvatarRenderer keeps displayPosByPlayer (remote-only; local stays controls.cursor) chasing player.avatarPos via pure smoothTowards(current,target,dtMs,tauMs,snapDist): k=1−e^(−dt/τ), τ=60ms (~81% of a 100ms net step), 300px snap guard (respawn/bench teleports), materialize-at-target on first sight (no (0,0) fly-in), dt clamped ≤100ms (tab-background stall), state GC'd with graphics + cleared on destroy. RENDER-ONLY — sim/snapshot/wire untouched. GATES: tsc clean; avatarRenderer.test.ts 13/13 (+6: converge/no-overshoot, dt-monotonic, frame-rate-independence 2×8ms≡1×16ms, ~100ms-step closes >75%, at-target exact, snap, dt≤0); FULL vitest 1188/1188; LOCAL playwright nplayer.spec chromium 3/3 (6-player render + 4p-FFA + real-WebRTC presence). Commit 10d1543 pushed.
priorities.objective: User: own cruiser smooth, enemy cruisers 'pixelated'. Root cause located: AvatarRenderer renders remote players at raw player.avatarPos which updates at ~10Hz (snapshot/intent cadence) → 100ms position steps. Add render-side dt-aware exponential chase (time-constant ~60ms, snap >300px for respawn/bench teleports). Local player path untouched.
priorities.testing: vitest: pure fn converges toward target, dt-aware, snaps beyond threshold, exact at target; manual smoke via existing e2e lanes (no spec asserts remote avatar pixel pos)

priorities.label: S81 P5 — top HUD (BETA badge, primitives legend, mute, gear) renders ABOVE the fog
priorities.check_method: S81 P5: legend/betaBadge/muteIndicator/settingsIcon still CREATED at bootstrap-top (handlers bind there) but addChild moved to AFTER fog + aboveFogLayer staging; relative order preserved (legend, beta, ♪-after-beta per S18 P1 note, ⚙); spawnerRing deliberately left early (board element — should be fogged); explanatory comments at both sites. VERIFIED LIVE in browser preview (session port 14857, then stopped): stage indices fog=7 < memory=8 < aboveFogLayer=9 < legend=10 < beta=11 < ♪=12 < ⚙=13, zero console errors. GATES: tsc clean; playwright fog.spec chromium 6/6 (fog mask invariants unaffected). Commit f9a7470 pushed.
priorities.objective: User: 'stuff in the top (like where it says beta or shows primitives) is hidden within the fog.' Root cause: legend/betaBadge/muteIndicator/settingsIcon are addChild'd BEFORE FogRenderer constructs (fog container sits above them). Move their stage adds to after aboveFogLayer. spawnerRing stays fogged (board element).
priorities.testing: tsc; fog e2e spec (fog.spec) local; visual order assertion via __SPARK__.app stage children if cheap

priorities.label: S81 P6 — Pac-Man hunter ~20% faster (MAX_SPEED 3.5→4.2, ACCEL 0.3→0.36, ratio preserved)
priorities.check_method: S81 P6: HUNTER_MAX_SPEED 3.5→4.2 + HUNTER_ACCEL 0.30→0.36 (×1.2 both; 0.0857 ratio + terminal-speed-under-cap headroom shape preserved → juke character unchanged, S76 P1 precedent); version-history comment updated. S78-lesson coupling check: hunter.spec catch wait is an UPPER bound (stationary target, 90s budget) — faster only catches sooner. GATES: tsc clean; full vitest green; LOCAL playwright hunter.spec chromium 1/1 (caught in 8s). Commit 34f9842 pushed.
priorities.objective: User: 'pacman should be about 20% faster moving.' Scale HUNTER_MAX_SPEED and HUNTER_ACCEL together by 1.2 (terminal speed 3.6 stays just under the 4.2 cap — same juke character, S76 P1 precedent).
priorities.testing: vitest (hunter tests import constants); hunter e2e spec uses score-injection seams (S78 lesson verified — no natural-speed timing coupling)

priorities.label: S81 P7 — poop falls 25% slower (POOP_FALL_SPEED 7→5.25) + answer the cruiser-poopy question in the report
priorities.check_method: S81 P7: POOP_FALL_SPEED 7→5.25 (−25%; binary-exact float, determinism unchanged); coupling check — unit collision tests are radius-tolerant (19px hit radius vs 1.75px landing shift), two exact-y comments rewritten to reference the constant; e2e injected-poop fall reaches its target prim in ~4 ticks (well inside the 2.5s window). Cruiser-poopy question answered honestly in the report: the slow debuff targets FREE sparks; the cruiser is cursor-bound (teleport-to-pointer) so slowing it = a movement-model feature — flagged as follow-up, NOT silently shipped (Rule 16). GATES: tsc clean; FULL vitest 1188/1188; LOCAL playwright seagull.spec chromium 1/1. Commit 1519a80 pushed.
priorities.objective: User: poops fall too fast to test getting hit. Slow fall 25%. ALSO: answer truthfully — the poopy-slow mechanic applies to FREE sparks (floating building blocks), NOT the player cruiser; the cruiser is cursor-bound (teleport-to-pointer) so slowing it = a movement-model feature, proposed as a follow-up, NOT silently shipped (Rule 16).
priorities.scope: src/constants.ts; seagull.test.ts if any test hardcodes ticks-to-floor
priorities.testing: vitest; seagull e2e spec checked for fall-time coupling before landing

reflexion_entries_to_archive: P1 #ownership-predicate-extraction: owner-only splat wipe shipped as a PURE predicate (canAvatarCleanSplat) absorbing the S80 bench gate + new ownership gate + radius — main.ts sweep shrank to one call and the whole eligibility contract became unit-testable (the S80 bench fix had lived untested inside main.ts). Ownership via ownerColor (not placedBy) so the rainbow shuffle (lockstep remap) and a future Steal both keep working — parity test locks it.

P2 #per-grab-timer #seam-audit-before-constants: 3s in-hand cook-off via carriedAtTick stamped on pickup / cleared on place+drop — per-grab semantics gives pass-it-on gameplay for free (re-grab restarts). Pre-checked every potato e2e carry window BEFORE landing the constant (S78 lesson): all carry legs sub-3s sim; the S75 bench spec (fuse 180 = hold 180) has an identical observable either way. Additive-optional serialization emitted only while CARRIED keeps FREE/ARMED snapshot bytes unchanged.

P3 #stateless-randomness #hash-not-rng: random poop intervals WITHOUT touching any RNG stream — pure mix32(seagullId, lastPoopTick) picks each gap; both inputs already rode save/load, so replay/reload determinism came for free with ZERO new state. LESSON: when a deterministic system needs 'randomness', hash state you already serialize before reaching for a new seeded stream (no serialization work, no byte-drift risk to the 5 locked spawner streams). Sized the e2e smoke wait to the new worst-case gap (48 ticks at degraded CI fps → 1.5s→2.5s) in the same commit.

P4 #render-side-smoothing #pure-helper-export: 'pixelated' enemy cruisers root-caused to raw 10Hz avatarPos rendering (vs the local cursor's per-frame glide) — fixed render-only with an exponential chase (k=1−e^(−dt/τ), τ=60ms, 300px snap guard, materialize-at-target) so sim/wire/determinism untouched. The S10 test-via-pure-helper-export pattern again: smoothTowards extracted, 6 tests incl. frame-rate independence (2×8ms ≡ 1×16ms).

P5 #construction-order-is-z-order #verify-in-live-browser: the fogged HUD was pure Pixi child-add order — the row was staged before FogRenderer existed. Fix = move addChild, keep creation (handlers bind early). Verified the REAL stage indices in a live preview (fog=7 < aboveFogLayer=9 < legend/beta/♪/⚙=10-13) instead of trusting the diff — for z-order bugs the browser IS the test.

P6 #ratio-preserving-tune: hunter +20% = both MAX_SPEED and ACCEL ×1.2 (0.0857 ratio + terminal-under-cap headroom preserved → juke character unchanged, only absolute speed). Confirmed the e2e catch wait is an UPPER bound (stationary target) so faster-only-catches-sooner — speed tunes are e2e-safe in that direction only.

P7 #scope-honesty-over-silent-feature: poop fall −25% shipped as asked; the user's real question ('does poop slow MY spark?') answered truthfully in the report — the debuff targets FREE sparks, the cruiser is cursor-bound (teleport-to-pointer) so slowing it is a movement-model feature needing its own design + netcode thought. Rule 16: flag the gap, don't silently expand scope into the input model.

Full local chromium e2e 36 pass/1 skip; vitest 1188/1188; build 534.5KB <550 charter. Carry-forward flagged to user: cruiser-poopy-slow (poop hitting the player avatar) does not exist by design — needs a movement-model decision if wanted.
